description: Spider mites are tiny arachnid pests that feed on plant cells. Heavy infestations can cause extensive leaf damage, reduced photosynthesis, plant stress, and yield loss.
symptoms: Fine yellow or white stippling on leaves
Leaves may develop a bronzed appearance
Fine webbing may appear on leaves and stems
Mites and eggs may be visible on leaf undersides with magnification
Severely damaged leaves may dry and fall

favorable_conditions.humidity: Low to moderate humidity often favors rapid population growth
favorable_conditions.additional_conditions: Hot, dry, dusty conditions and plant stress can favor infestations

immediate_actions: Remove heavily infested leaves in small plantings where practical
Use a strong water spray in suitable small-scale settings to dislodge mites
cultural_control: Avoid practices that destroy beneficial predators
biological_control: Consider commercially available predatory mites in suitable protected-crop systems

chemical_control.recommended: Insecticidal soaps, horticultural oils, or locally registered miticides may be considered depending on crop conditions and infestation severity.
chemical_control.warning: Spider mites can develop resistance rapidly. Confirm local product registration and rotate approved modes of action when miticides are necessary. Oils and soaps can cause plant injury under unsuitable conditions, so follow product labels.

severity_guidance.low: Small localized mite population with limited stippling. Monitor and conserve predators.
severity_guidance.moderate: Visible stippling across multiple leaves with increasing mite numbers. Apply integrated pest management.
severity_guidance.high: Heavy webbing, widespread bronzing, or leaf loss. Prompt control is required to prevent severe crop damage.

farmer_advice: Check the underside of leaves before confirming spider mites. Fine stippling combined with visible mites or webbing provides stronger diagnostic evidence than leaf discoloration alone.
disclaimer: Spider mites are pests, not plant pathogens. Nutrient disorders and other sucking pests can cause similar discoloration.
